findings.description: The memo header fields (TO, FROM, THROUGH, SUBJECT) in the PDF use a structured label-value layout with clear indentation alignment, while the HTML renders them as simple bold-label paragraphs without the aligned columnar structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows TO:, FROM:, THROUGH:, SUBJECT: as left-aligned labels with values indented to a consistent column position. HTML renders these as sequential lines without the tabular indentation alignment.

findings.description: Table 1 in the PDF has visible grid lines and borders, while the HTML version uses a different styling with more spacing between rows, but all data and structure are preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF Table 1 has traditional bordered table cells; HTML has a more open layout but same columns and data values (NO2, 1-hour, 158.99, 15.3, 174.3, 188, 93%).

findings.description: The footnotes (1) and (2) beneath Table 1 are present in both versions with the same content, but the HTML renders them in slightly different formatting with superscript handling.
findings.evidence: Both versions contain the same footnote text about model design value and monitor design value.

findings.description: The compliance demonstration disclaimer and cc: line appear at the bottom of page 1 in the HTML but span to page 2 in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF has the disclaimer text and 'cc: Tom Anderson' on a separate page 2, while HTML consolidates this onto the same scrollable page.

findings.description: Table A1 in the PDF is a compact bordered table, while the HTML renders it with more vertical spacing between rows, but all data values match across both versions.
findings.evidence: Both contain the same K1-K24 entries on the first visible portion with identical X-Utm, Y-Utm, Elevation, Stack Height, Temp, Velocity, Stack Diameter, and CAPped values.

findings.description: Table A2 title in the PDF references '1-hour SO2 and NO2 Modeled Emission Rates' but the table only shows NO2 column. The HTML version matches this same content.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show Table A2 with only NO2 (lb/hr) column, both titled referencing SO2 and NO2.

findings.description: The HTML has no heading elements (h1-h6) at all. The document lacks any heading hierarchy - the memorandum title, table titles, and section labels are all rendered as plain paragraphs with inline styling or bold text.
findings.evidence:
The document title 'DIVISION OF AIR QUALITY' and section labels like 'MEMORANDUM', 'Table 1.', 'Table A1.', 'Table A2.' are all rendered as

elements with tags rather than proper heading elements.

findings.description: The HTML contains a nested/duplicated <main> element, wrapping the entire content in two <main> tags. While this is a structural issue, the actual content does not appear to be duplicated.
